questions.prompt: Complete: "The editorial ___ that the minister had misled parliament, without stating it directly."
questions.explanation: The editorial (as writer/speaker) implies — it puts meaning in indirectly without stating it. The reader infers — takes meaning out from what they read. Inferred is therefore the wrong direction. Affected means to have an impact on; effected (as a verb) means to bring about — neither fits the meaning of suggesting something indirectly.

questions.prompt: Complete: "We need ___ volunteers for this project — not a constant stream, just someone willing to help now and again."
questions.explanation: "Continual" means recurring at intervals with breaks — fitting the context of helping "now and again". "Continuous" means without any interruption at all, which contradicts "not a constant stream". Historic means significant or landmark; principal means main — neither relates to frequency of volunteering.

options.text: There are fewer options than we expected.
We have less time to prepare than last year.
Less volunteers turned up than were promised.
Fewer errors were found in the second draft.

questions.explanation: "Volunteers" is a countable noun, so fewer is required: fewer volunteers. Less is correct only with uncountable nouns (less time, less water). The other three sentences all apply the rule correctly: fewer options (countable), less time (uncountable), fewer errors (countable).

questions.prompt: Complete: "The wine's subtle oak notes perfectly ___ the richness of the aged cheese."
questions.explanation: "Complemented" (verb from complement) means to go well with or complete — exactly the relationship between wine notes and cheese. "Complimented" means to pay verbal praise, which cannot apply to flavours. "Implied" is about indirect suggestion; "composed" is about making up a whole. The spelling difference (compliMENT vs compLEMENT) is the classic trap here.

questions.prompt:
Read: "She was ___ about the outcome — she had no stake in either side and simply wanted a fair process."
Which word BEST completes the sentence?
questions.explanation: "Disinterested" means impartial — having no personal stake in the outcome, which fits someone who simply wants fairness. "Uninterested" means bored or indifferent, which contradicts the active desire for a fair process. "Sensible" means showing good judgement but does not convey impartiality. "Notorious" means famous for something negative — entirely wrong here.

questions.prompt: Find the sentence with a COLLOCATION error.

options.text: The government pledged to raise awareness of the health risks.
She reached a decision after weighing all the evidence.
They did a historic mistake by ignoring the warning signs.
The team committed a serious foul in the closing minutes.

questions.explanation: "Did a historic mistake" contains two errors: the correct collocation is make a mistake (not do), and the correct adjective is historical (relating to the past) or simply a serious mistake. "Historic" means landmark or significant and does not collocate naturally with mistake. Raise awareness, reach a decision, and commit a foul are all standard collocations.
